AthleteGate.noActiveCoach: If you are an approved coach, pick an athlete in Athletes. If the account is awaiting platform approval, data modules stay disabled until enabled.
AthleteGate.noActiveCoachApproved: Pick an athlete from the Athletes list to open their data (calendar, health, nutrition…).

Pricing.paywallBannerBody: To use the Empathy platform you need an active plan or a complimentary access granted by an admin (testimonial / promo). Pick a plan below, or contact support if you believe you are entitled to free access.

Marketing.pitch: Empathy is a performance and physiology adaptation platform that drives adaptation through timing, stimuli, and nutrition. Measure change and bring you to real performance.
Marketing.card1Body: Sensors, imports, and lab data feed a canonical athlete state shared across modules.
Marketing.card2Body: Deterministic computation and a digital twin; AI interprets, orchestrates, and surfaces evidence.
Marketing.card3Body: Builder, calendar, nutrition, and health consume the same @empathy/contracts contracts.
Marketing.labBody: Connect your Garmin, Polar, or Wahoo device. Empathy reads every heartbeat, every watt, every meter and turns it into scientific analysis to improve your performance.
Marketing.howStep1Body: Sync your watch or bike computer. We support Garmin, Polar, Wahoo and FIT/TCX files.
Marketing.howStep2Body: Run, ride, ski, lift. Empathy collects every data point in the background without interrupting your flow.
Marketing.howStep3Body: Receive insights on physiology, biomechanics, nutrition and load. Adapt training based on real data.
Marketing.previewBody: Explore the preview dashboard to discover how Empathy turns raw data into actionable insights.

BuilderEngineGenerateSection.introStrength: The engine proposes the structure (exercise names), then the <cat>card on the EMPATHY catalog</cat> materializes with sets, reps, recoveries and images. Discipline: <disc>{sport}</disc>.
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.introTechnical: Focus on technique, tactics, patterns and modules: the pure-aerobic part stays in A · Aerobic. The builder estimates a <tss>TSS</tss> to compare with RPE / internal load. Discipline: <disc>{sport}</disc>.
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.introLifestyle: Presets for mobility, recovery, movement quality and light aerobic work (mind-body). Discipline: <disc>{sport}</disc>.
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.introAerobic: Aerobic macro: compact inputs. Output: blocks + exercises from the library, calibrated on the athlete's physiological profile and digital twin when available.
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.equipmentHint: Weights, bodyweight, cables, bands, machines. No chip = no strict filter.
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.executionStyleLabel: Execution style in the generated card
BuilderEngineGenerateSection.modularStructureHint: Align work phase, game context and athletic quality: the engine enriches cue and track (like V1 goals: offensive/defensive phase, patterns, technical module).

CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.title: How it works
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.subtitle: Moves, import formats and calendar data window
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.moveSessionBody: Drag a <plan>PLAN</plan> chip onto another day in the grid to move the session (same Builder structure, new date). Executed workouts (EXEC) do not move.
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.importFormatsBody: Auto mode: FIT workout files become calendar sessions (PLAN), recorded activities become EXEC. Structured sessions: ZWO, ERG, MRC or FIT workout — one session on the chosen day with a block chart like in the Builder. Executed tracks: FIT/FIT.GZ, CSV, JSON, TCX, GPX.
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.dataWindowBody: The loaded data includes a few days before and after the visible month, so sessions at the edges do not disappear from the grid.
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.warningNotRendered: warning · executed present but not rendered in grid
CalendarEngineDetailsAccordion.executedGapSummary: Executed in the month: {totalRows}. Visible EXEC chips: {renderedCount}.

CalendarFileImportSection.cardSubtitle: Auto: FIT workout → calendar (Planned); activity → Executed. Calendar: ZWO/ERG/MRC/CSV. Executed: recorded track.
CalendarFileImportSection.dayHintAuto: Auto: FIT/ZWO/ERG/MRC workout → Planned chip (Zwift/Rouvy export); FIT/TCX/GPX activity → Executed. Day = selected cell.
CalendarFileImportSection.dayHintExecuted: Recorded tracks only (Analyzer). The day is the one selected in the grid.
CalendarFileImportSection.dayHintPlanned: Tabular program or structured session on this day (Planned).
CalendarFileImportSection.formatHintPlanned: Tabular: CSV/JSON calendar export (multiple sessions). Structured: ZWO, ERG, MRC or FIT workout — a single session on the chosen day, with a block chart as in the Builder.
CalendarFileImportSection.formatHintExecuted: Executed: FIT/FIT.GZ, CSV, JSON, TCX, GPX. Saving uses the day indicated above (current cell if you do not change the date). Device: auto or manual.
CalendarFileImportSection.fallbackText: If the planned import fails, import the same file as an executed workout (track for Analyzer). Useful when the FIT does not convert well in Builder: the Analyzer uses the series as for executed ones (workout-only FIT files without records may have minimal charts).

BioenergeticTransparencyHubPageView.title: Bioenergetics

MetabolicPowerComponentsStackChart.cardAriaLabel: Model power breakdown: oxidative, PCr and glycolytic by duration
MetabolicPowerComponentsStackChart.title: Three metabolic components · power share by duration
MetabolicPowerComponentsStackChart.caption: P(t) = CP + W′/t with CP/W′ from work–time fit when R² is high. PCr: PPCr = min(W′/t, (EPCr/t)·e−t/τ). Glycolysis: hyperbolic share W′/t − PPCr + threshold-parallel share CP·f∥(t) (grows with log t). Oxidative = residual. Not lab V̇La.

The kJ columns are P·t at the row duration (energy in that bin): the PCr kJ do not stay the same as t varies (old model: fixed E/t).

HealthPageView.selectActiveAthlete: Select an active athlete to upload reports and view trends.
HealthPageView.pageDescription: Upload your reports and track trends over time.
HealthPageView.methodologyParagraph1: Every uploaded report is read and its values end up in a per-athlete archive. Cards and charts always use the numbers from the most recent report per type (blood, microbiota, epigenetics, hormones, inflammation, oxidative stress).
HealthPageView.methodologyParagraph2: The summary scores in «Health status» appear when they are present in the uploaded reports. Trends over time activate from the second compatible report onward: with a single report you still see all the values extracted in the «Health status» section.
HealthPageView.methodologyParagraph3: When the report is a photo or a PDF, extraction can propose values to confirm before they become final in the charts.

NutritionPageView.shellDescription: Meals, fueling and supplementation built on your training day: pick the day and generate the plan.
NutritionPageView.noActiveAthlete: No active athlete. If you are a coach, set the athlete in Athletes.
NutritionPageView.mealPlanHowTitle: How the meal plan is built
NutritionPageView.mealPlanHowBody: The day's requirement starts from basal metabolism, lifestyle and the cost of the selected day's workouts. The share allocated to meals is distributed according to the percentages in your profile (Profile → Diet) and each meal is assembled with real foods from the USDA database, respecting exclusions, preferences and the day's active metabolic pathways. The pre/intra/post-session part lives in the Fueling area, not in the meals.
NutritionPageView.adherenceAdaptationToggle: Use plan vs. intake comparison in the adaptation calculation.
NutritionPageView.adherenceAdaptationHint: Enable it only if diary and plan are filled in precisely: adherence gaps influence the nutrition dials.
NutritionPageView.fuelingHowTitle: How the fueling plan is built
NutritionPageView.fuelingHowBody: The pre/intra/post plan starts from the day's session: first the one planned in the calendar, failing that the imported executed one. Duration and estimated intensity determine hourly carbohydrates, fluids and sodium; we also estimate gut absorption, Cori return and glycogen depletion to build the session steps and charts.
NutritionPageView.integrationHowBody: The suggestions start from the day's active metabolic pathways (session, signals, diary) and from the nutrition levers. The product catalog is filtered on the brands and supplements in your profile; timing is expressed in qualitative half-life classes, not in medical dosages.
NutritionPageView.supplementsMatchingNote: Supplements and brands from the profile (CSV + supplement_config). Used for deterministic matching to the fueling catalog.
NutritionPageView.noProfileTokens: No profile tokens: catalog with default brands (Enervit, SiS, Maurten, +Watt, Powerbar).
NutritionPageView.predictorHowBody: The forecast estimates energy expenditure from sport, duration and intensity (% FTP from your physiological profile), compares it with the configured fueling and calculates the risk of glycogen depletion before the end of the event, with the suggested maximum sustainable pace.
NutritionPageView.diaryHowBody: The diary compares what you log with the targets of the selected day (the same as the meal plan). If you enable adaptation from adherence in the meal plan, the plan vs. intake comparison enters the adaptation calculation.
NutritionPageView.savePredictorFuelingHint: Persists predictor and fueling knobs to the profile (does not regenerate the plan).
NutritionPageView.predictorWhatIfSubtitle: Simulate a session or a race not yet scheduled: expenditure, CHO and glycogen risk. Does not change the plan.
NutritionPageView.engineDiagnosticsSubtitle: Engine parameters, data status and adherence — staff area

DashboardAthleteHubCard.title: Operational hub
DashboardAthleteHubCard.summaryLine: Summary from Supabase for the active athlete; training with calendar window (default −7 / +28 days). Same payload as the Physiology Bioenergetics page.
DashboardAthleteHubCard.bioenergeticsHeader: Bioenergetics · twin → adaptation loop → nutrition dial
DashboardAthleteHubCard.deltaHintTraces: Delta hint includes {count} recent coach traces.
DashboardAthleteHubCard.scoreFormulaExpectedZero: expected = 0: scorePct derives from clamped observed only (buildAdaptationGuidance implementation).
DashboardAthleteHubCard.adaptationTwinSource: <v>expected / observed</v> values from <s>athlete_memory.twin</s> (expectedAdaptation, realAdaptation; fallback adaptationScore).
DashboardAthleteHubCard.adaptationTrafficLightRule: Traffic light: green if score ≥ 75%; yellow 50–75% (suggested volume reduction 30–50%); red <50% (50–75%). See <s>lib/adaptation/adaptation-guidance.ts</s>.
DashboardAthleteHubCard.calendarLoopDivergence: <s>divergenceScore</s>: from twin if present, otherwise <m>clamp(|ΔTSS 7d planned − executed| × 0.5, 0, 100)</m> with Δ from <s>planned_workouts</s> vs <s>executed_workouts</s>.
DashboardAthleteHubCard.calendarLoopStatusAction: Status <val>{status}</val> and action <val>{action}</val> from rules in <s>adaptation-regeneration-loop.ts</s> (compliance, recovery, protective mode, divergence thresholds).
DashboardAthleteHubCard.nutritionDialBody:
Deterministic engine buildNutritionPerformanceIntegration: trainingEnergyScale = clamp(bioScale × opScale, 0.35, 1.02) then diary multipliers (adequacy), loop (×0.96 regenerate, ×0.985 watch), final clamp; intra CHO and protein bias from traffic light, bioenergetics, loop and diary. See lib/nutrition/performance-integration-scaler.ts.

Note: trainingEnergyScale is a recovery/bio indicator — the solver uses it for meal↔fueling timing and composition, not to reduce the total daily energy requirement (= BMR + lifestyle + planned training).
DashboardAthleteHubCard.crossModuleRowsBody: Strings from <s>buildOperationalDynamicsLines</s> (same hub API payload): adaptation, operational context{opctx}, loop, nutrition dial.
DashboardAthleteHubCard.physiologySources: Physiology sources: profile {profile} · metabolic lab {metabolic} · lactate {lactate} · max ox {maxox} · biomarkers {biomarkers}

FuelingSection.heading: Fueling
FuelingSection.intro: Pre, intra and post plan for the day's session (Builder planned takes priority; if missing, uses duration/TSS from the imported executed workout). Summary numbers first, expandable details when needed.
FuelingSection.intakeDescription: Confirm that you followed the plan (pre / intra / post) for {date}. The confirmation is saved and can support the plan vs actual comparison if you enable nutrition adherence on the meal plan.
FuelingSection.confirmIntake: I confirm fueling intake for this day
FuelingSection.noWorkoutNote: For the date above the module finds no workout in the loaded period: no row in <b1>Training → Calendar</b1> and no <b2>executed workout</b2> with that date. Fueling is tied to the <b3>day</b3>: move the date or add/import the session — this is not a Physiology problem.
FuelingSection.profileGapNote: Without the elements above (profile and physiology for fueling) we do not estimate CHO/h and lactate with made-up numbers. Fill them in under <b1>Profile</b1> and <b2>Physiology</b2>.
FuelingSection.dayWorkoutNote: On top of that you need a workout for that day (planned in the calendar or imported as executed), otherwise there is no session to compute pre/intra/post on.
FuelingSection.recoveryPoor: Low recovery: favor a simpler, progressive fueling approach, watch GI tolerance and avoid needless aggressiveness during the day.
FuelingSection.recoveryModerate: Intermediate recovery: keep an eye on fueling density, hydration and the post-workout window.
FuelingSection.substratesLine: Substrates (engine estimate): induced intensity ~{intensity}% FTP · lact ~{lact} g · Cori glucose ~{cori} g (net ~{coriNet} g) · exogenous CHO oxidized ~{exo} g · CHO available ~{choAvail} g · CHO energy share ~{share}% · gut pathway risk: {gutRisk} · blood delivery ~{delivery}%
FuelingSection.packageMeta: ~{duration} min · estimated intensity ~{intensity}% FTP · session CHO/h ~{cho} g/h
FuelingSection.stackIntroTraining: Your preferred brands' products by moment of the day, dosed on today's session CHO/h.
FuelingSection.stackIntroRest: No session today: daily and recovery products remain — no out-of-context intra dosing.
FuelingSection.glycogenIntakeLine: During the session you take in ~{intake} g of CHO and absorb ~{absorbed} g · gut risk: {gutRisk}.

HealthArchiveSection.panelsIntro: Panels from <c>biomarker_panels</c> for the active athlete
HealthArchiveSection.valuesExplanation: The cards and charts read the numbers from <c1>values</c1> of the most recent panel per type (e.g. <c>crp_mg_l</c>, <c>cortisol_am</c>, <c>firmicutes_pct</c>, <c>methylation_score</c>) and fall back to the <c>vlm_proposals</c> not yet confirmed.
HealthArchiveSection.noReadableNumbers: No report has readable numbers: upload an exam or apply a canonical seed.
HealthArchiveSection.openReviewsUpdateCanonical: Open reviews update the canonical state in the DB; until you confirm them, the numbers stay in «proposed» mode.
HealthArchiveSection.chartsAligned: The charts are aligned with the athlete's memory.
HealthArchiveSection.profileAthleteMismatch: Athlete linked to your profile: <c>{userProfileAthleteId}</c> — the athlete active in the UI does not match. Open Athletes / Access and select the one with the reports, or re-run the SQL seed on <c>{requestedAthleteId}</c>.
HealthArchiveSection.problemPersistsHint: If the problem persists, sign out and back in or verify that the selected athlete is the one with the reports in the database.
HealthArchiveSection.noReportsInArchive: No report in the archive for this athlete. Use «Upload exam» above, or apply the SQL seed on the same active <c>athlete_id</c>
HealthArchiveSection.noStructuredValue: No structured value in this panel.
HealthArchiveSection.proposedValuesToConfirm: {count} proposed values to confirm. Open the review to accept them.

HealthAreaCharts.endocrineRadarNoPanel: Upload a hormone report with structured numbers.
HealthAreaCharts.hormonesBarMapped: The bars use the mapped hormones (cortisol AM/PM, testosterone, TSH, free T3/T4). The raw values are in «Last uploaded report».
HealthAreaCharts.hormonesBarNoData: No structured hormone data — import a hormone report.
HealthAreaCharts.oxidativeRadarMapped: No marker mapped to the radar — the raw values are in «Last uploaded report».

HealthAreaCharts.demoUntilReport: — demo until a report with numbers is available
HealthAreaCharts.inflammationRadarMapped: No marker mapped to the PCR/IL-6 axes — the raw values are in «Last uploaded report».
HealthAreaCharts.inflammationRadarNoPanel: Upload an inflammation report for this chart.

IntegrationSection.integrationSubtitle: Metabolic pathways, KPIs, USDA and products — same signals as the module.
IntegrationSection.overviewBody: The numbers below derive from the same fueling levers: active metabolic pathways, daily signals, diary insight and operational constraints. Timing expressed as qualitative half-life classes.
IntegrationSection.signalModulates: . This signal modulates the training-nutrition levers but does not replace the physiological engines.
IntegrationSection.crossDomainRoadmapSummary: + Cross-domain roadmap · wired domains vs backlog
IntegrationSection.metabolicPathwaysSummary: + Metabolic pathways · substrates, cofactors, inhibitors, timing
IntegrationSection.fourColumns: Four color-coded columns: pathway/stimulus · signal source · substrate strategy · supplement support and attenuations.
IntegrationSection.noPathwayComputed: No pathway computed for {date}: add a planned session or check twin/physiology. The models (glycogen, redox, gut) appear when there are stimuli or signals.
IntegrationSection.operationalTiming: Operational timing — color per phase (acute pre · peri-session · early recovery · late recovery)
IntegrationSection.functionalFoodsSummary: Functional foods (vitamins, amino acids, cofactors) per nutrient
IntegrationSection.functionalFoodsIntro: For each nutrient linked to the active pathways you'll find curated examples and product search in the Meals tab, with a list of foods ordered by nutrient density: choose and cross-reference with your profile.
IntegrationSection.noFoodTarget: No food target yet: active metabolic pathways are needed (session + signals). The queries will appear here.
IntegrationSection.preWorkoutSubtitle: Pre-hydration, caffeine, moderate loads before the stimulus
IntegrationSection.intraWorkoutSubtitle: Gels, bars and drinks during the session (tolerance)
IntegrationSection.postWorkoutSubtitle: Recovery, protein, creatine — after the stimulus
IntegrationSection.noSupplementClassified: No supplement classified here from the current selection (primary bucket from catalog timing).

MealPlanSection.dayPlanDescription: Choose the day and generate the meal plan calibrated on profile, workouts and signals of the day.
MealPlanSection.lowMealsBudgetWarning: Warning — meal budget very low. Meal target ~{meals} kcal with estimated training ~{train} kcal: usually weight/height/date of birth are missing in the profile (BMR not computable). The breakfast/lunch/dinner percentages from the profile apply to that low total; check Profile and regenerate the plan after saving.
MealPlanSection.raceDayPreRaceNotice: Race day: pre-race meal ({meal}) at {time} — pasta/rice {carbs} g CHO/kg, grana cheese, oil 15 g. Meals before/during race → Fueling; solids in the afternoon/post-race.
MealPlanSection.snacksInTrainingWindow: Snacks in the training window ({slots}) → carbs during the session in the Fueling module, not as an extra solid meal.
MealPlanSection.sixMealsExpected: 6 meals expected: save Profile → Diet (Tuesday) with three snack % and regenerate the plan.
MealPlanSection.fewerSlotsThanDiet: Plan generated with fewer slots than Diet: use «Regenerate plan» to recompute.
MealPlanSection.noReadableSplit: No readable % split for {weekDayKey}: Profile → Diet (week_plan) with 6 meals and three snack %, then Save profile.

MetabolicLabDetailsSection.multiscaleSubtitle: Narrative and interpretive tags only: it does not modify your profile data
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.multiscaleNote: L1–L6 priorities and ontology nodes activated deterministically. The canonical numbers remain in the physiology / bioenergetics engines.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.howItWorksSubtitle: How to read the three analyses and what we save for you
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.metabolicProfile: Metabolic profile — From your power curve we derive thresholds (CP, FTP, LT), estimated VO₂max and the training zones with the predicted carbohydrate and fat consumption. When you save, the value stays on your profile and the next time you open it you find your latest analysis again.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.lactateAnalysis: Lactate analysis — Estimates how much fuel (carbohydrates) you use at a given intensity, how much you absorb and how much you reconvert. It helps you understand how to fuel during long efforts.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.oxidativeCapacity: Oxidative capacity — Measures how deeply you can use oxygen and where the limit lies (heart/blood, muscle, or aerobic ceiling). Useful for prolonged aerobic efforts.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.numbersUpdateNote: The numbers update on their own when you change the inputs; the history keeps every analysis you save, so you can compare your progress.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.validationConsoleNote: Internal validation console (visible only to coach/staff).
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.publishableRule: Rule: a value is publishable only if source-check valid + physiological range in literature + evidence available.
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.historySubtitle: Snapshot list + import into inputs — technical detail below
MetabolicLabDetailsSection.engineNote: The KPIs on the page use the current engine ({rev}). Below is the frozen snapshot at save time.
